name: codixing
description:
Code retrieval engine that saves your AI agent 73% of its token budget. Replaces grep with ranked, AST-aware search so models spend tokens reasoning, not reading. Before modifying code, Codixing tells Claude what will break — predicting impact across the dependency graph, finding affected tests, and tracing callers through the call hierarchy. Unlike text search, Codixing builds an AST-aware index with BM25 + semantic hybrid retrieval, dependency graphs with PageRank ranking, and trigram-accelerated grep — all running locally with zero cloud dependencies.

67 MCP tools cover the full development loop: search by symbol or natural language, trace dependencies, predict change impact, discover test coverage, review complexity, and navigate unfamiliar codebases. Five built-in slash commands (/codixing-setup, /codixing-explore, /codixing-review, /codixing-preflight, /codixing-release) provide guided workflows for project onboarding, architecture exploration, and dependency-aware code review. Dogfooding hooks automatically redirect Grep and Bash-invoked grep/rg/find to the Codixing CLI so agents always use the right tool. Tested at scale on the Linux kernel (63K C/H files, 84K-node graph). Runs on macOS and Linux; Windows support is available for the raw CLI/MCP binary but the plugin's bash-based dogfooding hooks are not currently tested on Windows.
